name: Sierras de Aldea del Rey — circular por el Cerro de la Higuera
region_intro: Hiking around Aldea Del Rey offers exploration of a unique volcanic landscape within the Campo de Calatrava region of Ciudad Real, Spain. The area is characterized by well-preserved Tertiary-era volcanic formations, diverse ecosystems, and the presence of the Jabalón River. Trails often lead through varied terrain, including areas with significant flora and fauna, providing opportunities to experience the natural richness of the region. Historical sites, such as medieval castles and archaeological ruins, are also integrated into the hiking experience. Best hiking trails around Aldea Del Rey The most popular hiking route is Archaeological site of Oreto y Zuqueca by the Río Jabalón — circular from Granátula de Calatrava, a 10.1 miles (16.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 10 minutes to complete. This route explores an ancient archaeological site alongside the Jabalón River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Los Castillos de los Cristianos, de Salvatierra y de Calatrava la Nueva — circular, a difficult 10.2 miles (16.5 km) path. This trail offers panoramic views from historical castles, including the Sacro Convento y Castillo de Calatrava La Nueva. Local hikers also love the Sierras de Aldea del Rey — circular por el Cerro de la Higuera, a 10.6 miles (17.0 km) trail leading through the Sierras and past Cerro de la Higuera, often completed in about 4 hours 39 minutes. Hiking around Aldea Del Rey is defined by volcanic formations, the Jabalón River, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Aldea Del Rey are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 6 reviews. More than 15 hikers have used komoot to explore Aldea Del Rey's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Aldea del Rey?
A: The hiking trails in Aldea del Rey traverse a unique volcanic landscape within the Campo de Calatrava region. You'll encounter varied terrain shaped by well-preserved Tertiary-era volcanic formations, alongside areas featuring diverse flora and fauna. Some routes follow the course of the Jabalón River, offering a mix of geological interest and riverside paths.

Q: Are there hiking routes suitable for beginners in Aldea del Rey?
A: While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in the immediate area, the region offers a good selection of moderate trails. These routes provide an accessible way to experience the landscape without extreme difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1183503474/archaeological-site-of-oreto-y-zuqueca-by-the-rio-jabalon-circular-from-granatula-de-calatrava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Archaeological site of Oreto y Zuqueca by the Río Jabalón — circular from Granátula de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 16.3 km path that explores ancient sites and the riverbank.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Aldea del Rey?
A: There are 8 hiking trails available around Aldea del Rey, offering various distances and difficulty levels to explore the region's unique volcanic landscapes and historical sites.

Q: What unique natural features can I discover while hiking in this region?
A: Aldea del Rey is part of the Volcanic Massif Natural Monument of Campo de Calatrava, meaning you can explore well-preserved Tertiary-era volcanic formations. The region also boasts diverse ecosystems, including the Bosque de los Enebros (Juniper Forest) and the Río Jabalón. In autumn, the area is known for an abundance of mushrooms.

Q: Are there any specific natural monuments to look out for?
A: Yes, the area features several notable natural monuments. You can explore the unique geological formations at &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3793255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervideros Baños del Barranco&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3495895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chorrillo Spring&lt;/a&gt;. The volcanic character of the region is also evident at sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1789437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Gordo Volcano&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7542220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Yozosa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The region around Aldea del Rey is rich in diverse flora and fauna. Hikers might spot deer, especially during the autumn rut, and various bird species such as golden eagles, black storks, orioles, and bee-eaters. The varied vegetation includes gall oaks, strawberry trees, and Montpellier maples.

Q: Are there historical landmarks or archaeological sites along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the hiking experience in Aldea del Rey is enriched by significant historical and archaeological sites. You can visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2713619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacro Convento y Castillo de Calatrava la Nueva&lt;/a&gt;, a medieval fortress offering panoramic views. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2713622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oreto and Zuqueca Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient Iberian settlement, is accessible on some routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1183503474/archaeological-site-of-oreto-y-zuqueca-by-the-rio-jabalon-circular-from-granatula-de-calatrava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Archaeological site of Oreto y Zuqueca by the Río Jabalón — circular from Granátula de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Aldea del Rey?
A: Yes, several circular hiking routes are available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1183445137/sierras-de-aldea-del-rey-circular-por-el-cerro-de-la-higuera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierras de Aldea del Rey — circular por el Cerro de la Higuera&lt;/a&gt; offers a 17 km loop through the local sierras. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1180593862/los-castillos-de-los-cristianos-de-salvatierra-y-de-calatrava-la-nueva-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Castillos de los Cristianos, de Salvatierra y de Calatrava la Nueva — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a challenging 16.5 km loop with historical castle views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Aldea del Rey?
A: The region's diverse flora and fauna, along with its unique volcanic landscape, make it appealing throughout the year. Autumn is particularly special due to the 'mushroom explosion' and the deer rut. Spring offers pleasant temperatures and blooming vegetation. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, while winters are generally mild enough for hiking.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Aldea del Rey?
A: While specific 'easy' routes are not listed, many of the moderate trails can be adapted for families, depending on the children's experience and stamina. The paths exploring the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2713622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oreto and Zuqueca Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt; or those along the Río Jabalón offer interesting points of discovery that can engage younger hikers.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Aldea del Rey?
A: The hiking routes in Aldea del Rey are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from 6 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique volcanic formations, the presence of the Jabalón River, and the integration of historical landmarks into the trails, providing a rich and varied outdoor experience.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads?
A: Information on specific parking facilities for each trailhead is not detailed in the guide. However, for routes starting near towns or popular attractions like the castles or archaeological sites, it is generally possible to find parking in the vicinity. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Are there any places to eat or stay near the hiking trails?
A: Aldea del Rey, as a town, offers local amenities including cafes and potentially accommodation options. While specific establishments along the trails are not listed, planning your hike to start or end in the town or a nearby village like Granátula de Calatrava would allow access to these services.
tips: This Church was founded by the Order of Calatrava under the invocation of San Jorge Mártir as patron saint of the town and of the Order. It acted as the chapel of the Palacio de la Clavería located behind the Temple. It is a Church from the beginnings of the Modern Age, with additions in all subsequent centuries. It is made up of a single nave covered by a flat ceiling and exposed wooden beams, three chapels on the Gospel side and one on the Epistle side covered by domes. It has a large presbytery and a rectangular apse covered by a crooked dome, a choir at the foot on Tuscan columns and a tower with a 17th-century base with mid-20th-century finials. On the main Altar there is an image of Saint George and below it a crucified Christ carved in wood.
When you walk through the fields that surround what is called the Sierra de Aldea del Rey, named after the town that is located to the east of this rounded system.

The Hermitage was called in ancient times Nª Sª del Val de Padilla, since it was the XIX Grand Master of the Order of Calatrava who donated the image. Its style is sober on the classic Latin Cross with a slender and well-ornamented transept.
Monument dedicated to all those who died for their ideals and for Spain.
